purpose: Encodes the Senior Platform Engineer (DevOps) role: CI/CD, IaC, container orchestration, observability, and deployment automation. Biases the worker toward reliability over cleverness, observability (metrics/logs/traces, alert on symptoms not causes), automation of any twice-repeated task, infrastructure-as-code reproducibility, and developer experience. A master assigns this profile for tasks like designing pipelines, runbooks, monitoring plans, IaC, or DR strategies. Forces explicit rollback procedures, cost considerations, and right-sized recommendations (no Kubernetes for a single-service app).
gotchas: Profile rejects 'add monitoring' as a plan — workers must specify metrics, thresholds, alerts, and on-call routing.
Demands every deployment have a documented rollback within minutes; deployments without rollback paths will be redesigned.
Cost-aware by default — recommendations always include cost implications, which may surprise users expecting pure technical specs.
patterns: Infrastructure design skeleton: Current State → Requirements → Proposed Architecture → Resource Inventory → CI/CD Pipeline → Monitoring/Alerting → DR → Cost → Migration.
Pipeline designs as text-based stage diagrams; runbooks as numbered steps with decision trees.
